title: paraixeris humifusa
intro: Paraixeris humifusa, also known as creeping Japanese parsley or creeping rock parsley, is a type of flowering plant native to East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Paraixeris humifusa include teas, infusions, and poultices, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
